title: rubus × adulteratus
intro: Rubus × adulteratus, a hybrid species of the Rubus genus, is a cross between the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and the blackberry (Rubus allegheniensis), resulting in a unique and potent plant with various medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it a potential remedy for various health issues, including digestive problems, arthritis, and cardiovascular diseases. The health benefits of Rubus × adulteratus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, making it a popular choice in herbal medicine. Rubus × adulteratus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or jam, and it is often consumed fresh or frozen to enjoy its flavor and medicinal benefits.
